{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e60fd76d6c0a420cbfa99e98","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scoped Meilisearch key rotation (plan §13.21).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Implements the leader-based rotation sequence:\n4\t//! 1. Mint new scoped key via Meilisearch `POST /keys`\n5\t//! 2. Write new generation to Redis hash\n6\t//! 3. Wait for all live pods to observe new generation (beacon check)\n7\t//! 4. Drain wait for stragglers\n8\t//! 5. `DELETE /keys/{previous_uid}` on all Meilisearch nodes\n9\t//! 6. Clear previous from Redis hash\n10\t\n11\tuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n12\tuse miroir_core::task_store::{RedisTaskStore, SearchUiScopedKey, TaskStore};\n13\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n14\tuse std::time::Duration;\n15\tuse tracing::{info, warn, error};\n16\t\n17\tuse crate::routes::indexes::MeilisearchClient;\n18\t\n19\t/// State for the scoped key rotation background task.\n20\t#[derive(Clone)]\n21\tpub struct ScopedKeyRotationState {\n22\t    pub config: Arc<MiroirConfig>,\n23\t    pub redis: RedisTaskStore,\n24\t    pub pod_id: String,\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t/// Response body for the manual rotation endpoint.\n28\t#[derive(serde::Serialize)]\n29\tpub struct RotateScopedKeyResponse {\n30\t    pub status: String,\n31\t    pub index_uid: String,\n32\t    pub generation: i64,\n33\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n34\t    pub previous_uid_revoked: Option<String>,\n35\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n36\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\t/// Request body for the manual rotation endpoint.\n40\t#[derive(serde::Deserialize)]\n41\tpub struct RotateScopedKeyRequest {\n42\t    /// If true, bypass the timing gate and rotate immediately.\n43\t    #[serde(default)]\n44\t    pub force: bool,\n45\t}\n46\t\n47\t/// Run the background scoped key rotation loop.\n48\tpub async fn run_scoped_key_rotator(state: ScopedKeyRotationState) {\n49\t    if !state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n50\t        return;\n51\t    }\n52\t\n53\t    let check_interval = Duration::from_secs(3600); // Check every hour\n54\t    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(check_interval);\n55\t\n56\t    info!(\"scoped key rotation background task started\");\n57\t\n58\t    loop {\n59\t        interval.tick().await;\n60\t\n61\t        // Refresh our own pod presence heartbeat\n62\t        if let Err(e) = state.redis.register_pod_presence(&state.pod_id) {\n63\t            warn!(pod_id = %state.pod_id, error = %e, \"failed to register pod presence\");\n64\t        }\n65\t\n66\t        // Check each index for rotation need with a per-index leader lease\n67\t        let indexes = discover_scoped_indexes(&state).await;\n68\t        for index_uid in &indexes {\n69\t            let lease_scope = format!(\"search_ui_key_rotation:{}\", index_uid);\n70\t            let lease_now = now_ms();\n71\t            let lease_ttl_ms = (state.config.leader_election.lease_ttl_s as i64) * 1000;\n72\t            let expires_at = lease_now + lease_ttl_ms;\n73\t\n74\t            match state.redis.try_acquire_leader_lease(&lease_scope, &state.pod_id, expires_at, lease_now) {\n75\t                Ok(true) => {}\n76\t                Ok(false) => continue, // Another pod holds the lease for this index\n77\t                Err(e) => {\n78\t                    warn!(index = %index_uid, error = %e, \"failed to acquire rotation leader lease\");\n79\t                    continue;\n80\t                }\n81\t            }\n82\t\n83\t            if let Err(e) = check_and_rotate(&state, index_uid, false).await {\n84\t                error!(index = %index_uid, error = %e, \"scoped key rotation failed\");\n85\t            }\n86\t\n87\t            // Renew the lease for this index\n88\t            let _ = state.redis.renew_leader_lease(\n89\t                &lease_scope,\n90\t                &state.pod_id,\n91\t                now_ms() + lease_ttl_ms,\n92\t            );\n93\t        }\n94\t    }\n95\t}\n96\t\n97\t/// Check if a scoped key needs rotation and perform it if so.\n98\tpub async fn check_and_rotate(\n99\t    state: &ScopedKeyRotationState,\n100\t    index_uid: &str,\n101\t    force: bool,\n102\t) -> Result<RotateScopedKeyResponse, String> {\n103\t    let current = state.redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(index_uid)\n104\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"redis read failed: {e}\"))?;\n105\t\n106\t    // Timing gate check (skip if force)\n107\t    if !force {\n108\t        if !should_rotate(&current, &state.config) {\n109\t            return Ok(RotateScopedKeyResponse {\n110\t                status: \"skipped\".into(),\n111\t                index_uid: index_uid.into(),\n112\t                generation: current.as_ref().map(|k| k.generation).unwrap_or(0),\n113\t                previous_uid_revoked: None,\n114\t                error: None,\n115\t            });\n116\t        }\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    // Step 1: Mint new scoped key via Meilisearch POST /keys\n120\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(state.config.node_master_key.clone());\n121\t    let (new_key, new_uid) = mint_scoped_key(&client, &state.config, index_uid).await?;\n122\t\n123\t    // Step 2: Write new generation to Redis\n124\t    let new_generation = current.as_ref().map(|k| k.generation + 1).unwrap_or(1);\n125\t    let previous_uid = current.as_ref().map(|k| k.primary_uid.clone());\n126\t    let previous_key = current.as_ref().map(|k| k.primary_key.clone());\n127\t\n128\t    let scoped_key = SearchUiScopedKey {\n129\t        index_uid: index_uid.into(),\n130\t        primary_key: new_key.clone(),\n131\t        primary_uid: new_uid.clone(),\n132\t        previous_key,\n133\t        previous_uid: previous_uid.clone(),\n134\t        rotated_at: now_ms(),\n135\t        generation: new_generation,\n136\t    };\n137\t\n138\t    state.redis.set_search_ui_scoped_key(&scoped_key)\n139\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"redis write failed: {e}\"))?;\n140\t\n141\t    info!(\n142\t        index = %index_uid,\n143\t        generation = new_generation,\n144\t        \"new scoped key minted, waiting for pod observation\"\n145\t    );\n146\t\n147\t    // Step 3: Observe our own beacon immediately\n148\t    state.redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n149\t        &state.pod_id,\n150\t        index_uid,\n151\t        new_generation,\n152\t    ).map_err(|e| format!(\"beacon write failed: {e}\"))?;\n153\t\n154\t    // Step 4: Wait for drain period and check beacons\n155\t    let drain_s = state.config.search_ui.scoped_key_rotation_drain_s;\n156\t    let drain_deadline = tokio::time::Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(drain_s);\n157\t\n158\t    let mut previous_revoked: Option<String> = None;\n159\t\n160\t    loop {\n161\t        // Get live pods\n162\t        let live_pods = state.redis.get_live_pods()\n163\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"get_live_pods failed: {e}\"))?;\n164\t\n165\t        // Check if all live pods have observed the new generation\n166\t        let (all_observed, unobserved) = state.redis.check_scoped_key_observation(\n167\t            index_uid,\n168\t            new_generation,\n169\t            &live_pods,\n170\t        ).map_err(|e| format!(\"beacon check failed: {e}\"))?;\n171\t\n172\t        if all_observed {\n173\t            info!(\n174\t                index = %index_uid,\n175\t                generation = new_generation,\n176\t                \"all live pods observed new generation, revoking previous key\"\n177\t            );\n178\t\n179\t            // Step 6: Delete previous key from Meilisearch and clear from Redis\n180\t            if let Some(ref prev_uid) = previous_uid {\n181\t                if let Err(e) = revoke_previous_key(&client, &state.config, prev_uid).await {\n182\t                    warn!(previous_uid = %prev_uid, error = %e, \"failed to revoke previous key, will retry\");\n183\t                } else {\n184\t                    previous_revoked = Scoped Meilisearch key rotation (plan §13.21).\n//!\n//! Implements the leader-based rotation sequence:\n//! 1. Mint new scoped key via Meilisearch `POST /keys`\n//! 2. Write new generation to Redis hash\n//! 3. Wait for all live pods to observe new generation (beacon check)\n//! 4. Drain wait for stragglers\n//! 5. `DELETE /keys/{previous_uid}` on all Meilisearch nodes\n//! 6. Clear previous from Redis hash\n\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse miroir_core::task_store::{RedisTaskStore, SearchUiScopedKey, TaskStore};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tracing::{info, warn, error};\n\nuse crate::routes::indexes::MeilisearchClient;\n\n/// State for the scoped key rotation background task.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct ScopedKeyRotationState {\n    pub config: Arc<MiroirConfig>,\n    pub redis: RedisTaskStore,\n    pub pod_id: String,\n}\n\n/// Response body for the manual rotation endpoint.\n#[derive(serde::Serialize)]\npub struct RotateScopedKeyResponse {\n    pub status: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub generation: i64,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub previous_uid_revoked: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Request body for the manual rotation endpoint.\n#[derive(serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct RotateScopedKeyRequest {\n    /// If true, bypass the timing gate and rotate immediately.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub force: bool,\n}\n\n/// Run the background scoped key rotation loop.\npub async fn run_scoped_key_rotator(state: ScopedKeyRotationState) {\n    if !state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n        return;\n    }\n\n    let check_interval = Duration::from_secs(3600); // Check every hour\n    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(check_interval);\n\n    info!(\"scoped key rotation background task started\");\n\n    loop {\n        interval.tick().await;\n\n        // Refresh our own pod presence heartbeat\n        if let Err(e) = state.redis.register_pod_presence(&state.pod_id) {\n            warn!(pod_id = %state.pod_id, error = %e, \"failed to register pod presence\");\n        }\n\n        // Check each index for rotation need with a per-index leader lease\n        let indexes = discover_scoped_indexes(&state).await;\n        for index_uid in &indexes {\n            let lease_scope = format!(\"search_ui_key_rotation:{}\", index_uid);\n            let lease_now = now_ms();\n            let lease_ttl_ms = (state.config.leader_election.lease_ttl_s as i64) * 1000;\n            let expires_at = lease_now + lease_ttl_ms;\n\n            match state.redis.try_acquire_leader_lease(&lease_scope, &state.pod_id, expires_at, lease_now) {\n                Ok(true) => {}\n                Ok(false) => continue, // Another pod holds the lease for this index\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(index = %index_uid, error = %e, \"failed to acquire rotation leader lease\");\n                    continue;\n                }\n            }\n\n            if let Err(e) = check_and_rotate(&state, index_uid, false).await {\n                error!(index = %index_uid, error = %e, \"scoped key rotation failed\");\n            }\n\n            // Renew the lease for this index\n            let _ = state.redis.renew_leader_lease(\n                &lease_scope,\n                &state.pod_id,\n                now_ms() + lease_ttl_ms,\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Check if a scoped key needs rotation and perform it if so.\npub async fn check_and_rotate(\n    state: &ScopedKeyRotationState,\n    index_uid: &str,\n    force: bool,\n) -> Result<RotateScopedKeyResponse, String> {\n    let current = state.redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(index_uid)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"redis read failed: {e}\"))?;\n\n    // Timing gate check (skip if force)\n    if !force {\n        if !should_rotate(&current, &state.config) {\n            return Ok(RotateScopedKeyResponse {\n                status: \"skipped\".into(),\n                index_uid: index_uid.into(),\n                generation: current.as_ref().map(|k| k.generation).unwrap_or(0),\n                previous_uid_revoked: None,\n                error: None,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Step 1: Mint new scoped key via Meilisearch POST /keys\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(state.config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let (new_key, new_uid) = mint_scoped_key(&client, &state.config, index_uid).await?;\n\n    // Step 2: Write new generation to Redis\n    let new_generation = current.as_ref().map(|k| k.generation + 1).unwrap_or(1);\n    let previous_uid = current.as_ref().map(|k| k.primary_uid.clone());\n    let previous_key = current.as_ref().map(|k| k.primary_key.clone());\n\n    let scoped_key = SearchUiScopedKey {\n        index_uid: index_uid.into(),\n        primary_key: new_key.clone(),\n        primary_uid: new_uid.clone(),\n        previous_key,\n        previous_uid: previous_uid.clone(),\n        rotated_at: now_ms(),\n        generation: new_generation,\n    };\n\n    state.redis.set_search_ui_scoped_key(&scoped_key)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"redis write failed: {e}\"))?;\n\n    info!(\n        index = %index_uid,\n        generation = new_generation,\n        \"new scoped key minted, waiting for pod observation\"\n    );\n\n    // Step 3: Observe our own beacon immediately\n    state.redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n        &state.pod_id,\n        index_uid,\n        new_generation,\n    ).map_err(|e| format!(\"beacon write failed: {e}\"))?;\n\n    // Step 4: Wait for drain period and check beacons\n    let drain_s = state.config.search_ui.scoped_key_rotation_drain_s;\n    let drain_deadline = tokio::time::Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(drain_s);\n\n    let mut previous_revoked: Option<String> = None;\n\n    loop {\n        // Get live pods\n        let live_pods = state.redis.get_live_pods()\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"get_live_pods failed: {e}\"))?;\n\n        // Check if all live pods have observed the new generation\n        let (all_observed, unobserved) = state.redis.check_scoped_key_observation(\n            index_uid,\n            new_generation,\n            &live_pods,\n        ).map_err(|e| format!(\"beacon check failed: {e}\"))?;\n\n        if all_observed {\n            info!(\n                index = %index_uid,\n                generation = new_generation,\n                \"all live pods observed new generation, revoking previous key\"\n            );\n\n            // Step 6: Delete previous key from Meilisearch and clear from Redis\n            if let Some(ref prev_uid) = previous_uid {\n                if let Err(e) = revoke_previous_key(&client, &state.config, prev_uid).await {\n                    warn!(previous_uid = %prev_uid, error = %e, \"failed to revoke previous key, will retry\");\n                } else {\n                    previous_revoked = Some(prev_uid.clone());\n                    // Clear previous from Redis\n                    if let Err(e) = state.redis.clear_scoped_key_previous(index_uid) {\n                        warn!(error = %e, \"failed to clear previous key from redis\");\n                    }\n                    info!(index = %index_uid, previous_uid = %prev_uid, \"previous scoped key revoked\");\n                }\n            }\n\n            return Ok(RotateScopedKeyResponse {\n                status: \"rotated\".into(),\n                index_uid: index_uid.into(),\n                generation: new_generation,\n                previous_uid_revoked: previous_revoked,\n                error: None,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Not all pods have caught up yet\n        if tokio::time::Instant::now() >= drain_deadline {\n            warn!(\n                index = %index_uid,\n                generation = new_generation,\n                unobserved = ?unobserved,\n                \"drain wait expired, {} pods still unobserved — will retry on next tick\",\n                unobserved.len()\n            );\n\n            return Ok(RotateScopedKeyResponse {\n                status: \"drain_pending\".into(),\n                index_uid: index_uid.into(),\n                generation: new_generation,\n                previous_uid_revoked: None,\n                error: Some(format!(\n                    \"drain wait expired: {} pods unobserved: {}\",\n                    unobserved.len(),\n                    unobserved.join(\", \")\n                )),\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Wait before rechecking (every 10 seconds)\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(10)).await;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Check if rotation should happen based on timing gate.\nfn should_rotate(current: &Option<SearchUiScopedKey>, config: &MiroirConfig) -> bool {\n    let Some(key) = current else {\n        // No key exists yet — need to mint initial key\n        return true;\n    };\n\n    let max_age_ms = (config.search_ui.scoped_key_max_age_days as i64) * 24 * 3600 * 1000;\n    let rotate_before_ms = (config.search_ui.scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days as i64) * 24 * 3600 * 1000;\n    let age = now_ms() - key.rotated_at;\n\n    // Rotate if key has been alive long enough that it will expire within rotate_before_days\n    age >= (max_age_ms - rotate_before_ms)\n}\n\n/// Mint a new scoped Meilisearch key via POST /keys on all nodes.\npub async fn mint_scoped_key(\n    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n    config: &MiroirConfig,\n    index_uid: &str,\n) -> Result<(String, String), String> {\n    let description = format!(\"miroir search-ui scoped key for index {}\", index_uid);\n    let body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"description\": description,\n        \"actions\": [\"search\"],\n        \"indexes\": [index_uid],\n        \"expiresAt\": null,\n    });\n\n    let mut created_key: Option<String> = None;\n    let mut created_uid: Option<String> = None;\n    let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n    for node in &config.nodes {\n        match client.post_raw(&node.address, \"/keys\", &body).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if created_key.is_none() {\n                    let resp: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&text)\n                        .map_err(|e| format!(\"parse key response: {e}\"))?;\n                    created_key = resp.get(\"key\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(String::from);\n                    created_uid = resp.get(\"uid\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(String::from);\n                }\n            }\n            Ok((status, _text)) => {\n                errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {}\", node.id, status));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", node.id, e));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let key = created_key.ok_or_else(|| format!(\"failed to mint key on any node: {}\", errors.join(\"; \")))?;\n    let uid = created_uid.ok_or_else(|| String::from(\"key created but no uid returned\"))?;\n\n    Ok((key, uid))\n}\n\n/// Revoke a previous scoped key from all Meilisearch nodes.\npub async fn revoke_previous_key(\n    client: &MeilisearchClient,\n    config: &MiroirConfig,\n    previous_uid: &str,\n) -> Result<(), String> {\n    let path = format!(\"/keys/{}\", previous_uid);\n    let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n    for node in &config.nodes {\n        match client.delete_raw(&node.address, &path).await {\n            Ok((_status, _text)) if _status >= 200 && _status < 300 => {}\n            Ok((status, _text)) => {\n                // 404 is fine — key was already revoked or never existed\n                if status != 404 {\n                    errors.push(format!(\"{}: HTTP {}\", node.id, status));\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                errors.push(format!(\"{}: {}\", node.id, e));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    if errors.is_empty() {\n        Ok(())\n    } else {\n        Err(format!(\"partial revoke failure: {}\", errors.join(\"; \")))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Discover which indexes have scoped keys (or should have them).\n/// For now, we look at existing keys in Redis. New indexes get initial\n/// keys on their first search request.\nasync fn discover_scoped_indexes(state: &ScopedKeyRotationState) -> Vec<String> {\n    // Scan for existing scoped key hashes\n    state.redis.list_scoped_key_indexes()\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n}\n\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn should_rotate_no_key() {\n        let config = MiroirConfig::default();\n        assert!(should_rotate(&None, &config));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn should_rotate_old_key() {\n        let config = MiroirConfig {\n            search_ui: miroir_core::config::SearchUiConfig {\n                scoped_key_max_age_days: 60,\n                scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days: 30,\n                ..Default::default()\n            },\n            ..MiroirConfig::default()\n        };\n\n        // Key created 31 days ago — should rotate (max_age - rotate_before = 30 days)\n        let key = SearchUiScopedKey {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            primary_key: \"key\".into(),\n            primary_uid: \"uid\".into(),\n            previous_key: None,\n            previous_uid: None,\n            rotated_at: now_ms() - (31 * 24 * 3600 * 1000),\n            generation: 1,\n        };\n\n        assert!(should_rotate(&Some(key), &config));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn should_not_rotate_fresh_key() {\n        let config = MiroirConfig {\n            search_ui: miroir_core::config::SearchUiConfig {\n                scoped_key_max_age_days: 60,\n                scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days: 30,\n                ..Default::default()\n            },\n            ..MiroirConfig::default()\n        };\n\n        // Key created 10 days ago — should NOT rotate yet\n        let key = SearchUiScopedKey {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            primary_key: \"key\".into(),\n            primary_uid: \"uid\".into(),\n            previous_key: None,\n            previous_uid: None,\n            rotated_at: now_ms() - \"catalog\", false)\n405\t        .await\n406\t        .expect(\"check\");\n407\t    assert_eq!(result.status, \"skipped\");\n408\t\n409\t    // With force: should rotate\n410\t    let result = scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&state, \"catalog\", true)\n411\t        .await\n412\t        .expect(\"forced rotation\");\n413\t\n414\t    assert_eq!(result.status, \"rotated\");\n415\t    assert_eq!(result.generation, 2);\n416\t    assert_eq!(\n417\t        result.previous_uid_revoked,\n418\t        Some(\"fresh-uid-300\".into())\n419\t    );\n420\t\n421\t    let sk =        .try_acquire_leader_lease(scope_beta, \"pod-a\", now + 30000, now)\n500\t        .unwrap();\n501\t    assert!(acquired, \"pod-a should acquire lease for beta\");\n502\t\n503\t    // Pod-b P10.5 Scoped Meilisearch key rotation acceptance tests (plan §13.21).\n//!\n//! Tests:\n//! 1. Full rotation on 3-pod deployment: zero 403 responses during overlap\n//! 2. Kill one pod mid-rotation: leader waits drain, then retries\n//! 3. `force: true` manual rotation: old key revoked immediately\n//! 4. Timing gate: rotation skipped when key is fresh\n//! 5. Per-index leader lease scoping\n//! 6. Pod restart skips old UID entirely (missing-peer tolerance)\n\nuse miroir_core::config::{MiroirConfig, NodeConfig, SearchUiConfig};\nuse miroir_core::task_store::{RedisTaskStore, SearchUiScopedKey, TaskStore};\nuse miroir_proxy::routes::indexes::MeilisearchClient;\nuse miroir_proxy::scoped_key_rotation::{\n    self, RotateScopedKeyRequest, RotateScopedKeyResponse, ScopedKeyRotationState,\n};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse testcontainers::runners::AsyncRunner;\nuse testcontainers_modules::redis::Redis;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn make_config(node_addresses: Vec<String>, search_ui: SearchUiConfig) -> MiroirConfig {\n    let nodes: Vec<NodeConfig> = node_addresses\n        .into_iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .map(|(i, addr)| NodeConfig {\n            id: format!(\"node-{i}\"),\n            address: addr,\n            replica_group: 0,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirConfig {\n        master_key: \"test-master-key\".into(),\n        node_master_key: \"test-node-master-key\".into(),\n        shards: 64,\n        replication_factor: 1,\n        replica_groups: 1,\n        nodes,\n        search_ui,\n        ..MiroirConfig::default()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a RedisTaskStore from a testcontainers Redis instance.\nasync fn redis_store() -> RedisTaskStore {\n    let node = Redis::default();\n    let container = node.start().await.expect(\"start redis\");\n    let port = container\n        .get_host_port_ipv4(6379)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"get port\");\n    let url = format!(\"redis://localhost:{port}\");\n    RedisTaskStore::open(&url).await.expect(\"redis connect\")\n}\n\n/// Seed a scoped key into Redis (simulating a previous rotation).\nfn seed_scoped_key(\n    redis: &RedisTaskStore,\n    index: &str,\n    primary_key: &str,\n    primary_uid: &str,\n    previous_key: Option<&str>,\n    previous_uid: Option<&str>,\n    generation: i64,\n    rotated_at_ms_ago: i64,\n) {\n    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap()\n        .as_millis() as i64;\n\n    let key = SearchUiScopedKey {\n        index_uid: index.into(),\n        primary_key: primary_key.into(),\n        primary_uid: primary_uid.into(),\n        previous_key: previous_key.map(String::from),\n        previous_uid: previous_uid.map(String::from),\n        rotated_at: now - rotated_at_ms_ago,\n        generation,\n    };\n    redis.set_search_ui_scoped_key(&key).expect(\"seed scoped key\");\n}\n\n/// Simulate pod observation beacon.\nfn observe_pod(redis: &RedisTaskStore, pod_id: &str, index: &str, generation: i64) {\n    redis\n        .observe_search_ui_scoped_key(pod_id, index, generation)\n        .expect(\"observe pod\");\n}\n\n/// Register pod presence in live_pods sorted set.\nfn register_pod(redis: &RedisTaskStore, pod_id: &str) {\n    redis.register_pod_presence(pod_id).expect(\"register pod\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 1: Full rotation on 3-pod deployment — zero 403 during overlap\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: Full rotation with 3 pods. All pods observe the new generation\n/// before the old key is revoked — no 403 window.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_rotation_zero_403_during_overlap() {\n    let redis = redis_store().await;\n\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    // Setup: seed an existing scoped key (generation 1, rotated 35 days ago)\n    seed_scoped_key(\n        &redis,\n        \"products\",\n        \"old-key-value\",\n        \"old-key-uid-001\",\n        None,\n        None,\n        1,\n        35 * 24 * 3600 * 1000,\n    );\n\n    // All 3 pods registered as alive\n    register_pod(&redis, \"pod-a\");\n    register_pod(&redis, \"pod-b\");\n    register_pod(&redis, \"pod-c\");\n\n    // All 3 pods observing generation 1\n    observe_pod(&redis, \"pod-a\", \"products\", 1);\n    observe_pod(&redis, \"pod-b\", \"products\", 1);\n    observe_pod(&redis, \"pod-c\", \"products\", 1);\n\n    // Mock: POST /keys returns new key on both Meilisearch nodes\n    let new_key_response = json!({\n        \"key\": \"new-key-value\",\n        \"uid\": \"new-key-uid-002\"\n    });\n    let mock_post1 = server1\n        .mock(\"POST\", \"/keys\")\n        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(new_key_response.to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n    let mock_post2 = server2\n        .mock(\"POST\", \"/keys\")\n        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(new_key_response.to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Mock: DELETE /keys/old-key-uid-001 on both nodes (revocation after drain)\n    let mock_delete1 = server1\n        .mock(\"DELETE\", \"/keys/old-key-uid-001\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n    let mock_delete2 = server2\n        .mock(\"DELETE\", \"/keys/old-key-uid-001\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config = make_config(\n        vec![server1.url(), server2.url()],\n        SearchUiConfig {\n            scoped_key_max_age_days: 60,\n            scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days: 30,\n            scoped_key_rotation_drain_s: 1, // 1s drain for fast test\n            ..SearchUiConfig::default()\n        },\n    );\n\n    let state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n        config: std::sync::Arc::new(config),\n        redis: redis.clone(),\n        pod_id: \"pod-a\".into(),\n    };\n\n    // Pod-a observes new generation immediately during rotation.\n    // Simulate pods b and c observing the new generation after the leader writes it.\n    let redis_bg = redis.clone();\n    let observe_bg = tokio::spawn(async move {\n        for _ in 0..50 {\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n            if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis_bg.get_search_ui_scoped_key(\"products\") {\n                if sk.generation == 2 {\n                    observe_pod(&redis_bg, \"pod-b\", \"products\", 2);\n                    observe_pod(&redis_bg, \"pod-c\", \"products\", 2);\n                    return;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        panic!(\"timed out waiting for generation 2\");\n    });\n\n    let result = scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&state, \"products\", false)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"rotation should succeed\");\n\n    observe_bg.await.unwrap();\n\n    // Rotation should complete with old key revoked\n    assert_eq!(result.status, \"rotated\");\n    assert_eq!(result.generation, 2);\n    assert_eq!(result.previous_uid_revoked, Some(\"old-key-uid-001\".into()));\n\n    // Verify old key is no longer in Redis\n    let sk = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(\"products\").unwrap().unwrap();\n    assert!(sk.previous_key.is_none());\n    assert!(sk.previous_uid.is_none());\n    assert_eq!(sk.primary_key, \"new-key-value\");\n    assert_eq!(sk.primary_uid, \"new-key-uid-002\");\n\n    mock_post1.assert_async().await;\n    mock_post2.assert_async().await;\n    mock_delete1.assert_async().await;\n    mock_delete2.assert_async().await;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 2: Kill one pod mid-rotation — leader waits drain, then retries\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: During rotation, one pod (pod-z) never observes the new generation.\n/// The leader waits the drain period and returns drain_pending status.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_kill_pod_mid_rotation_drain_pending() {\n    let redis = redis_store().await;\n\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    seed_scoped_key(\n        &redis,\n        \"orders\",\n        \"old-key\",\n        \"old-uid-100\",\n        None,\n        None,\n        1,\n        40 * 24 * 3600 * 1000,\n    );\n\n    // 3 pods alive\n    register_pod(&redis, \"pod-x\");\n    register_pod(&redis, \"pod-y\");\n .mock(\"POST\", \"/keys\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(new_key_resp.to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config = make_config(\n        vec![server1.url(), server2.url()],\n        SearchUiConfig {\n            scoped_key_max_age_days: 60,\n            scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days: 30,\n            scoped_key_rotation_drain_s: 2, // 2s drain\n            ..SearchUiConfig::default()\n        },\n    );\n\n    let state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n        config: std::sync::Arc::new(config),\n        redis: redis.clone(),\n        pod_id: \"pod-x\".into(),\n    };\n\n    // pod-x observes gen 2 immediately; only pod-y also observes.\n    // pod-z is \"dead\" and never observes.\n    let    .expect(\"rotation attempt\");\n\n    observe_bg.await.unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(result.status, \"drain_pending\");\n    assert!(\n        result.error.as_ref().unwrap().contains(\"pod-z\"),\n        \"error should mention unobserved pod: {:?}\",\n        result.error\n    );\n\n    // Verify: old key is still present (not revoked)\n    let sk = ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 3: force=true manual rotation bypasses timing gate\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: Manual rotation with force=true mints a new key even when the\n/// current key is fresh (1 day old, well within the 30-day window).\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_force_rotation_bypasses_timing_gate() {\n    let redis = redis_store().await;\n\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let .mock(\"DELETE\", \"/keys/fresh-uid-300\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config =    .expect(\"check\");\n    assert_eq!(result.status, \"skipped\");\n\n    // With force: should rotate\n    let result = scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&state, \"catalog\", true)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"forced rotation\");\n\n    assert_eq!(result.status, \"rotated\");\n    assert_eq!(result.generation, 2);\n    assert_eq!(\n        result.previous_uid_revoked,\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 4: Timing gate — fresh key is not rotated\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: A key that is only 10 days old (max_age=60, rotate_before=30)\n/// is NOT rotated under the normal timing gate.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_timing_gate_skips_fresh_key() {\n    let redis = redis_store().await;\n\n    seed_scoped_key(\n        &redis,\n        \"items\",\n        \"young-key\",\n        \"young-uid-400\",\n        None,\n        None,\n        3,\n        10 * 24 * 3600 * 1000, // 10 days old\n    );\n\n    let config = make_config(\n        vec![\"http://unused:7700\".into()],\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 5: Per-index leader lease scoping\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: The leader lease scope includes the index name, allowing\n/// different pods to lead rotation for different indexes.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_per_index_leader_lease() {\n    let redis = redis_store().await;\n\n    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap()\n        .as_millis() as i64;\n\n    // Pod-a acquires lease for index \"alpha\"\n    let scope_alpha = \"search_ui_key_rotation:alpha\";\n    let acquired = redis\n        .try_acquire_leader_lease(scope_alpha, \"pod-a\", now + 30000, now)\n        .unwrap();\n    assert!(acquired, \"pod-a should acquire lease for alpha\");\n\n    // Pod-a acquires lease for index \"beta\"\n    let scope_beta = \"search_ui_key_rotation:beta\";\n    let acquired = redis\n        .try_acquire_leader_lease(scope_beta, \"pod-a\", now + 30000, now)\n        .unwrap();\n    assert!(acquired, \"pod-a should acquire lease for beta\");\n\n    // Pod-b cannot acquire alpha (pod-a holds it)\n    let acquired = redis\n        .try_acquire_leader_lease(scope_alpha, \"pod-b\", now + 30000, now)\n        .unwrap();\n    assert!(!acquired, \"pod-b should NOT steal alpha from pod-a\");\n\n    // Pod-b cannot acquire beta (pod-a holds it)\n    let acquired = redis\n        .try_acquire_leader_lease(scope_beta, \"pod-b\", now + 30000, now)\n        .unwrap();\n    assert!(!acquired, \"pod-b should NOT steal beta from pod-a\");\n\n    // But pod-b can acquire gamma (no one holds it)\n    let scope_gamma = \"search_ui_key_rotation:gamma\";\n    let acquired = redis\n        .try_acquire_leader_lease(scope_gamma, \"pod-b\", now + 30000, now)\n        .unwrap();\n    assert!(acquired, \"pod-b should acquire lease for gamma\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 6: Pod restart skips old UID entirely .unwrap();\n    assert!(all_observed, \"new pod should observe generation 2\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 7: Initial key minting (no crate::config::{ConfigError, MiroirConfig};\n\npub fn validate(cfg: &MiroirConfig) -> Result<(), ConfigError> {\n    // replication_factor > 1 requires redis backend for HA\n    if cfg.replication_factor > 1 && cfg.task_store.backend == \"sqlite\" {\n        return Err(ConfigError::Validation(\n            \"replication_factor > 1 requires task_store.backend = 'redis' (SQLite is single-writer)\".into(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // replica_groups > 1 requires redis backend\n    if cfg.replica_groups > 1 && cfg.task_store.backend == \"sqlite\" {\n        return Err(ConfigError::Validation(\n            \"replica_groups > 1 requires task_store.backend = 'redis' (SQLite is single-writer)\"\n                .into(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Nodes must belong to a valid replica group\n    if cfg.replica_groups > 0 {\n        for node in &cfg.nodes {\n            if node.replica_group >= cfg.replica_groups {\n                return Err(ConfigError::Validation(format!(\n                    \"node '{}' has replica_group={} but only {} groups exist (0..{})\",\n                    node.id,\n                    node.replica_group,\n                    cfg.replica_groups,\n                    cfg.replica_groups - 1\n                )));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Node IDs must be unique\n    let mut seen_ids = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n    for node in &cfg.nodes {\n        if !seen_ids.insert(&node.id) {\n            return Err(ConfigError::Validation(format!(\n                \"duplicate node id: '{}'\",\n                node.id\n            )));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // HPA enabled requires redis backend\n    if cfg.hpa.enabled && cfg.task_store.backend == \"sqlite\" {\n        return Err(ConfigError::Validation(\n            \"hpa.enabled = true requires task_store.backend = 'redis'\".into(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Search UI scoped_key timing validation\n    if cfg.search_ui.enabled {\n        let max_age = cfg.search_ui.scoped_key_max_age_days;\n        let rotate_before = cfg.search_ui.scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days;\n        if rotate_before >= max_age {\n            return Err(ConfigError::Validation(format!(\n                \"search_ui.scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days ({}) must be strictly less than scoped_key_max_age_days ({})\",\n                rotate_before, max_age\n            )));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // CDC overflow = redis requires redis backend\n    if cfg.cdc.enabled && cfg.cdc.buffer.overflow == \"redis\" && cfg.task_store.backend != \"redis\" {\n        return Err(ConfigError::Validation(\n            \"cdc.buffer.overflow = 'redis' requires task_store.backend = 'redis'\".into(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Search UI rate_limit.backend = redis requires redis task store (when multi-pod)\n    if cfg.search_ui.enabled\n        && cfg.search_ui.rate_limit.backend == \"redis\"\n        && ));\n    }\n\n    // Leader election should be enabled when replica_groups > 1\n    if cfg.replica_groups > 1 && !cfg.leader_election.enabled {\n        return Err(ConfigError::Validation(\n            \"leader_election.enabled must be true when replica_groups > 1\".into(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Tenant affinity dedicated_groups must be within valid range\n    if cfg.tenant_affinity.enabled {\n        for g in &cfg.tenant_affinity.dedicated_groups {\n            if *g >= cfg.replica_groups {\n                return Err(ConfigError::Validation(format!(\n                    \"tenant_affinity.dedicated_groups contains {} but only {} groups (0..{})\",\n                    g,\n                    cfg.replica_groups,\n                    cfg.replica_groups - 1\n                )));\n            }\n        }\n        for (tenant, group) in &cfg.tenant_affinity.static_map {\n            if *group >= cfg.replica_groups {\n                return Err(ConfigError::Validation(format!(\n                    \"tenant_affinity.static_map: tenant '{}' maps to group {} but only {} groups (0..{})\",\n                    tenant,\n                    group,\n                    cfg.replica_groups,\n                    cfg.replica_groups - 1\n                )));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Shadow targets must have valid sample_rate\n    if cfg.shadow.enabled {\n        for target in &cfg.shadow.targets {\n            if target.sample_rate <= 0.0 || target.sample_rate > 1.0 {\n                return Err(ConfigError::Validation(format!(\n                    \"shadow target '{}' has invalid sample_rate={} (must be 0 < rate <= 1)\",\n                    target.name, target.sample_rate\n                )));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Server port must be non-zero\n    if cfg.server.port == 0 {\n        return Err(ConfigError::Validation(\n            \"server.port must be non-zero\".into(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // shards must be non-zero\n    if cfg.shards == 0 {\n        return Err(ConfigError::Validation(\"shards must be non-zero\".into()));\n    }\n\n    // replication_factor must be > 0\n    if cfg.replication_factor == 0 {\n        return Err(ConfigError::Validation(\n            \"replication_factor must be > 0\".into(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // value in &cfg.admin_ui.csp_overrides.script_src {\n        if value == \"*\" {\n            return Err(ConfigError::Validation(\n                \"admin_ui.csp_overrides.script_src cannot contain healthy node, using cache if within TTL.\n81\t    pub async fn get_version(&self) -> Result<String, StatusCode> {\n82\t        // Check cache first\n83\t        {\n84\t            let cache = self.version_cache.read().await;\n85\t            let last_update = self.last_cache_update.read().await;\n86\t            if let (Some(ref cached), Some(last)) = (cache.as_ref(), last_update.as_ref()) {\n87\t                if last.elapsed().as_secs() < self.cache_ttl_secs {\n88\t                    return Ok((**cached).clone());\n89\t                }\n90\t            }\n91\t        }\n92\t\n93\t .await;\n106\t\n107\t            if let Ok(resp) = response {\n108\t                if resp.status().is_success() {\n109\t                    if let Ok(body) = resp.text().await {\n110\t Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n120\t    }\n121\t}\n122\t\n123\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n124\t// Local Rate Limiter (for single-pod deployments)\n125\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n126\t\n127\t/// In-memory rate limiter for admin login (local backend only).\n128\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n129\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n130\tpub struct LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n131\t    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalAdminRateLimiterInner>>,\n132\t}\n133\t\n134\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n135\tstruct LocalAdminRateLimiterInner {\n136\t    /// Map of IP -> (request_timestamps_ms, failed_count, backoff_until_ms)\n137\t    state: HashMap<String, LocalRateLimitState>,\n138\t}\n139\t\n140\t#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\n141\tstruct LocalRateLimitState {\n142\t    /// Timestamps of recent requests (for sliding window)\n143\t    request_timestamps_ms: Vec<i64>,\n144\t    /// Consecutive failed login attempts\n145\t    failed_count: u32,\n146\t    /// Unix timestamp (ms) when backoff expires\n147\t    backoff_until_ms: Option<i64>,\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\timpl LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n151\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n152\t        Self {\n153\t            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalAdminRateLimiterInner::default())),\n154\t        }\n155\t    }\n156\t\n157\t    /// Check rate limit and exponential backoff.\n158\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n159\t    pub fn check(\n160\t        &self,\n161\t        ip: &str,\n162\t        limit: u64,\n163\t        window_ms: u64,\n164\t        failed_threshold: u32,\n165\t        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n166\t        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n167\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n168\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n169\t        let now = now_ms();\n170\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n171\t\n172\t        // Check if we're in backoff mode\n173\t        if let Some(backoff_until) = state.backoff_until_ms {\n174\t            if backoff_until > now {\n175\t                let wait_seconds = ((backoff_until - now) / 1000) as u64;\n176\t                return (false, Some(wait_seconds));\n177\t            }\n178\t            // Backoff expired, clear it\n179\t            state.backoff_until_ms = None;\n180\t        }\n181\t\n182\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n183\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n184\t\n185\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n186\t        if state.request_timestamps_ms.len() >= limit as usize {\n187\t            // Enter backoff mode after threshold consecutive failures\n188\t            let failed = state.failed_count + 1;\n189\t            state.failed_count = failed;\n190\t\n191\t            if failed >= failed_threshold {\n192\t                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((failed - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7)); // Cap at 2^7 = 128x\n193\t                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n194\t                state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n195\t                return (false, Some(backoff_seconds));\n196\t            }\n197\t\n198\t            return (false, None);\n199\t        }\n200\t\n201\t        // Record this request\n202\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.push(now);\n203\t        (true, None)\n204\t    }\n205\t\n206\t    /// Reset rate limit and backoff state on successful login.\n207\t    pub fn reset(&self, ip: &str) {\n208\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n209\t        inner.state.remove(ip);\n210\t    }\n211\t\n212\t    /// Record a failed login attempt (for backoff calculation).\n213\t    pub fn record_failure(&self, ip: &str, failed_threshold: u32, backoff_start_minutes: u64, backoff_max_hours: u64) -> Option<u64> {\n214\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n215\t        let now = now_ms();\n216\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n217\t\n218\t        state.failed_count += 1;\n219\t\n220\t        if state.failed_count >= failed_threshold {\n221\t            let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((state.failed_count - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7));\n222\t            let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n223\t            state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n224\t            return Some(backoff_seconds);\n225\t        }\n226\t\n227\t        None\n228\t    }\n229\t}\n230\t\n231\timpl Default for LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n232\t    fn default() -> Self {\n233\t        Self::new()\n234\t    }\n235\t}\n236\t\n237\t/// Get current time in milliseconds since Unix epoch.\n238\tfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n239\t    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n240\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n241\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n242\t        .as_millis() as i64\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t/// Shared application state for admin endpoints.\n246\t#[derive(Clone)]\n247\tpub struct AppState {\n248\t    pub config: Arc<MiroirConfig>,\n249\t    pub topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n250\t    pub ready: Arc<RwLock<bool>>,\n251\t    pub metrics: super::super::middleware::Metrics,\n252\t    pub version_state: VersionState,\n253\t    pub task_registry: Arc<TaskRegistryImpl>,\n254\t    pub redis_store: Option<RedisTaskStore>,\n255\t    pub pod_id: String,\n256\t    pub seal_key: SealKey,\n257\t    pub local_rate_limiter: LocalAdminRateLimiter,\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\timpl AppState {\n261\t    pub fn new(\n262\t        config: MiroirConfig,\n263\t        metrics: super::super::middleware::Metrics,\n264\t        seal_key: SealKey,\n265\t    ) -> Self {\n266\t        Self::with_redis(config, metrics, None, \"unknown\".into(), seal_key)\n267\t    }\n268\t\n269\t    pub fn with_redis(\n270\t        config: MiroirConfig,\n271\t        metrics: super::super::middleware::Metrics,\n272\t        redis_store: Option<RedisTaskStore>,\n273\t        pod_id: String,\n274\t        seal_key: SealKey,\n275\t    ) -> Self {\n276\t        // Build initial topology from config\n277\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(\n278\t            config.shards,\n279\t            config.replica_groups,\n280\t            config.replication_factor as usize,\n281\t        );\n282\t\n283\t        for node_config in &config.nodes {\n284\t            let node = Node::new(\n285\t                NodeId::new(node_config.id.clone()),\n286\t                node_config.address.clone(),\n287\t                node_config.replica_group,\n288\t            );\n289\t            // Start nodes in Joining state - health checker will promote to Active\n290\t            topology.add_node(node);\n291\t        }\n292\t\n293\t        let version_state = VersionState::new(\n294\t            config.node_master_key.clone(),\n295\t            config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect(),\n296\t        );\n297\t\n298\t        // Select task registry backend based on config\n299\t        let task_registry = match config.task_store.backend.as_str() {\n300\t            \"redis\" if redis_store.is_some() => {\n301\t                let store = redis_store.as_ref().unwrap().clone();\n302\t                store.migrate().expect(\"Redis migration failed\");\n303\t                TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(Arc::new(store))\n304\t            }\n305\t            \"sqlite\" if !config.task_store.path.is_empty() => {\n306\t                TaskRegistryImpl::sqlite(&config.task_store.path)\n307\t                    .expect(\"Failed to open SQLite task store\")\n308\t            }\n309\t            _ => TaskRegistryImpl::in_memory(),\n310\t        };\n311\t\n312\t        Self {\n313\t            config: Arc::new(config),\n314\t            topology: Arc::new(RwLock::new(topology)),\n315\t            ready: Arc::new(RwLock::new(false)),\n316\t            metrics,\n317\t            version_state,\n318\t            task_registry: Arc::new(task_registry),\n319\t            redis_store,\n320\t            pod_id,\n321\t            seal_key,\n322\t            local_rate_limiter: LocalAdminRateLimiter::new(),\n323\t        }\n324\t    }\n325\t\n326\t    /// Mark the service as ready (all nodes reachable).\n327\t    pub async fn mark_ready(&self) {\n328\t        *self.ready.write().await = true;\n329\t        info!(\"Service marked as ready\");\n330\t    }\n331\t\n332\t    /// Check if a covering quorum is reachable.\n333\t    pub async fn check_covering_quorum(&self) -> bool {\n334\t        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n335\t        let node_map = topo.node_map();\n336\t\n337\t        // For each replica group, check if we have enough healthy nodes\n338\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n339\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n340\t            let required = (topo.rf() + 1) / 2; // Simple majority for quorum\n341\t            if healthy.len() < required {\n342\t                return false;\n343\t            }\n344\t        }\n345\t\n346\t        true\n347\t    }\n348\t}\n349\t\n350\t/// Response for GET /_miroir/topology (plan §10 JSON shape).\n351\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n352\tpub struct TopologyResponse {\n353\t    pub shards: u32,\n354\t    pub replication_factor: u32,\n355\t    pub nodes: Vec<NodeInfo>,\n356\t    pub degraded_node_count: u32,\n357\t    pub rebalance_in_progress: bool,\n358\t    pub fully_covered: bool,\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t/// Per-node information in the topology response.\n362\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n363\tpub struct NodeInfo {\n364\t    pub id: String,\n365\t    pub address: String,\n366\t    pub status: String,\n367\t    pub shard_count: u32,\n368\t    pub last_seen_ms: u64,\n369\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n370\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n371\t}\n372\t\n373\t/// Response for GET /_miroir/shards.\n374\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n375\tpub struct ShardsResponse {\n376\t    pub shards: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>, // shard_id -> list of node IDs\n377\t}\n378\t\n379\t/// GET /_miroir/topology — full cluster state per plan §10.\n380\tpub async fn get_topology<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<TopologyResponse>, StatusCode>\n381\twhere\n382\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n383\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n384\t{\n385\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n386\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n387\t\n388\t    // Count degraded nodes\n389\t    let degraded_count = topo.nodes().filter(|n| !n.is_healthy()).count() as u32;\n390\t\n391\t    // Build node info list\n392\t    let nodes: Vec<NodeInfo> = topo\n393\t        .nodes()\n394\t        .map(|n| NodeInfo {\n395\t            id: n.id.as_str().to_string(),\n396\t            address: n.address.clone(),\n397\t            status: format!(\"{:?}\", n.status).to_lowercase(),\n398\t            shard_count: 0, // TODO: compute from routing table\n399\t            last_seen_ms: 0, // TODO: track last health check time\n400\t            error: None,     // TODO: populate from last health check error\n401\t        })\n402\t        .collect();\n403\t\n404\t    // Check if fully covered\n405\t    let fully_covered = degraded_count == 0;\n406\t\n407\t    let response = TopologyResponse {\n408\t        shards: topo.shards,\n409\t        replication_factor: topo.rf() as u32,\n410\t        nodes,\n411\t        degraded_node_count: degraded_count,\n412\t        rebalance_in_progress: false, // TODO: track rebalance state\n413\t        fully_covered,\n414\t    };\n415\t\n416\t    Ok(Json(response))\n417\t}\n418\t\n419\t/// GET /_miroir/shards — shard → node mapping table.\n420\tpub async fn get_shards<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<ShardsResponse>, StatusCode>\n421\twhere\n422\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n423\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n424\t{\n425\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n426\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n427\t    let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n428\t\n429\t    // Build shard -> node mapping using rendezvous hash\n430\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n431\t        let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n432\t\n433\t        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n434\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n435\t            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n436\t            for node_id in assigned {\n437\t                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n438\t            }\n439\t        }\n440\t\n441\t        shards.insert(shard_id.to_string(), node_ids);\n442\t    }\n443\t\n444\t    Ok(Json(ShardsResponse { shards }))\n445\t}\n446\t\n447\t/// GET /_miroir/ready — readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\n448\tpub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\n449\twhere\n450\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n451\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n452\t{\n453\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n454\t    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n455\t\n456\t    if ready {\n457\t        Ok(\"\")\n458\t    } else {\n459\t        // Not yet marked ready - check if covering quorum exists\n460\t        let has_quorum = state.check_covering_quorum().await;\n461\t        if has_quorum {\n462\t            // Auto-mark ready on first successful quorum check\n463\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n464\t            Ok(\"\")\n465\t        } else {\n466\t            Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n467\t        }\n468\t    }\n469\t}\n470\t\n471\t/// GET /_miroir/metrics — admin-key-gated Prometheus metrics.\n472\tpub async fn get_metrics<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Response\n473\twhere\n474\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n475\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n476\t{\n477\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n478\t    match state.metrics.encode_metrics() {\n479\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics.into_response(),\n480\t        Err(e) => {\n481\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n482\t            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()\n483\t        }\n484\t    }\n485\t}\n486\t\n487\t/// POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key — manual rotation trigger.\n488\t///\n489\t/// Admin-gated endpoint that initiates a scoped key rotation for the given index.\n490\t/// Set `force: true` in the request body to bypass the timing gate.\n491\tpub async fn rotate_scoped_key_handler<S>(\n492\t    State(state): State<S>,\n493\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n494\t    Json(body): Json<RotateScopedKeyRequest>,\n495\t) -> Result<Json<RotateScopedKeyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)>\n496\twhere\n497\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n498\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n499\t{\n500\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n501\t\n502\t    let redis = app_state.redis_store.clone().ok_or_else(|| {\n503\t        (\n504\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n505\t            \"scoped key rotation requires Redis task store\".into(),\n506\t        )\n507\t    })?;\n508\t\n509\t    if !app_state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n510\t        return Err((\n511\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n512\t            \"search_ui is not enabled\".into(),\n513\t        ));\n514\t    }\n515\t\n516\t    let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n517\t        config: app_state.config.clone(),\n518\t        redis,\n519\t        pod_id: app_state.pod_id.clone(),\n520\t    };\n521\t\n522\t    info!(\n523\t        index = %index,\n524\t        force = body.force,\n525\t        pod_id = %app_state.pod_id,\n526\t        \"manual scoped key rotation triggered\"\n527\t    );\n528\t\n529\t    match scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&rotation_state, &index, body.force).await {\n530\t        Ok(response) => Ok(Json(response)),\n531\t        Err(e) => {\n532\t            error!(index = %index, error = %e, \"manual scoped key rotation failed\");\n533\t            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))\n534\t        }\n535\t    }\n536\t}\n537\t\n538\t/// Parse a rate limit string like \"10/minute\" into (limit, window_seconds).\n539\tfn parse_rate_limit(s: &str) -> Result<(u64, u64), String> {\n540\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.split('/').collect();\n541\t    if parts.len() != 2 {\n542\t        return Err(format!(\"invalid rate limit format: '{}', expected 'N/UNIT'\", s));\n543\t    }\n544\t    let limit: u64 = parts[0].parse()\n545\t        .map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid limit number: '{}'\", parts[0]))?;\n546\t    let window_seconds = match parts[1] {\n547\t        \"second\" | \"s\" => 1,\n548\t        \"minute\" | \"m\" => 60,\n549\t        \"hour\" | \"h\" => 3600,\n550\t        \"day\" | \"d\" => 86400,\n551\t        unit => return Err(format!(\"invalid time unit: '{}', expected second/minute/hour/day\", unit)),\n552\t    };\n553\t    Ok((limit, window_seconds))\n554\t}\n555\t\n556\t/// Generate a random session ID.\n557\tfn generate_session_id() -> String {\n558\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 24];\n559\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n560\t    hex::encode(&bytes)\n561\t}\n562\t\n563\t/// POST /_miroir/admin/login — admin login with rate limiting and exponential backoff.\n564\t///\n565\t/// Request body:\n566\t/// ```json\n567\t/// { \"admin_key\": \"...\" }\n568\t/// ```\n569\t///\n570\t/// On success, sets a `miroir_admin_session` cookie and returns:\n571\t/// ```json\n572\t/// { \"success\": true }\n573\t/// ```\n574\t///\n575\t/// Rate limiting (per source IP):\n576\t/// - 10 requests per minute (configurable via `admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip`)\n577\t/// - After 5 consecutive failed attempts, exponential backoff applies:\n578\t///   - 10m, 20m, 40m, ... up to 24h cap\n579\t///\n580\t/// Successful login resets both the rate limit counter and backoff state.\n581\tpub async fn admin_login<S>(\n582\t    State(state): State<S>,\n583\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n584\t    Json(body): Json<AdminLoginRequest>,\n585\t) -> Response\n586\twhere\n587\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n588\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n589\t{\n590\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n591\t\n592\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n593\t    let source_ip = headers\n594\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n595\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n596\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n597\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n598\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n599\t        .trim()\n600\t        .to_string();\n601\t\n602\t    // Parse rate limit config\n603\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n604\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n605\t        Err(e) => {\n606\t            error!(error = %e, \"invalid admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config\");\n607\t            return (\n608\t                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n609\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n610\t                    success: false,\n611\t                    message: Some(\"Rate limit configuration error\".into()),\n612\t                }),\n613\t            ).into_response();\n614\t        }\n615\t    };\n616\t\n617\t    // Check rate limit and backoff\n618\t    let backend = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n619\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n620\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n621\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n622\t                Ok((allowed, wait_seconds)) => {\n623\t                    if !allowed {\n624\t                        if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n625\t                            warn!(\n626\t                                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n627\t                                wait_seconds = ws,\n628\t                                \"admin login rate limited (backoff)\"\n629\t                            );\n630\t                            return (\n631\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n632\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n633\t                                    success: false,\n634\t                                    message: Some(format!(\n635\t                                        \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n636\t                                        ws\n637\t                                    )),\n638\t                                }),\n639\t                            ).into_response();\n640\t                        } else {\n641\t                            return (\n642\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n643\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n644\t                                    success: false,\n645\t                                    message: Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into()),\n646\t                                }),\n647\t                            ).into_response();\n648\t                        }\n649\t                    }\n650\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n651\t                }\n652\t                Err(e) => {\n653\t                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to check admin login rate limit\");\n654\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n655\t                }\n656\t            }\n657\t        }\n658\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n659\t        // Local backend rate limiting\n660\t        let (allowed, wait_seconds) = state.local_rate_limiter.check(\n661\t            &source_ip,\n662\t            limit,\n663\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n664\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold,\n665\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes,\n666\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours * 60,\n667\t        );\n668\t        if !allowed {\n669\t            warn!(\n670\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n671\t                wait_seconds = ?wait_seconds,\n672\t                \"admin login rate limited (local backend)\"\n673\t            );\n674\t            return (\n675\t                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n676\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n677\t                    success: false,\n678\t                    message: if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n679\t                        Some(format!(\n680\t                            \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n681\t                            ws\n682\t                        ))\n683\t                    } else {\n684\t                        Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into())\n685\t                    },\n686\t                }),\n687\t            ).into_response();\n688\t        }\n689\t    }\n690\t\n691\t    // Verify admin_key (constant-time comparison to prevent timing side-channels)\n692\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n693\t    if body.admin_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(state.config.admin.api_key.as_bytes()).into() {\n694\t        // Successful login - reset rate limit counters\n695\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n696\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n697\t                if let Err(e) = redis.reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip) {\n698\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to reset admin login rate limit\");\n699\t                }\n700\t            }\n701\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n702\t            state.local_rate_limiter.reset(&source_ip);\n703\t        }\n704\t\n705\t        // Generate session ID and seal it\n706\t        let session_id
